home.seoTitle: Free Digital Library for Teacher Education
home.seoDescription: Free textbooks, teaching resources, and research reports from Phnom Penh Teacher Education College — read online or download in Khmer and English.
home.headline: The Digital Library for the Next Generation of Teachers
home.description: Textbooks, research papers, and teaching resources — carefully curated, open and free for every student and educator.
home.heroSubtitle: Access thousands of books, research papers, and educational resources.
home.discoverLatest: Discover the latest research insights, library announcements, and educational resources from our community.
home.searchPlaceholder: Search title, author, ISBN, or topic
home.prompt1: Find a book about teaching methods…
home.askHint: Search e-books, physical books, and theses — or press / to start typing.
home.constellationHint: Focus the search field to see the collection light up.
home.bentoEbooksBody: Read anywhere — every e-book is free for PTEC students and teachers, on any device.
home.aboutLibraryBody: PTEC was established in 2017 by merging two teacher-training centers, with support from JICA. The library is home to the Department of Educational Research and Library — built for Cambodia's next generation of teachers.
home.researchBody: Read PTEC's research bulletin and reports by our own lecturers and student teachers.
home.aboutLede: The Heart of Learning and Research for Excellence in 21st Century Teacher Education
home.aboutSupport: The Department of Educational Research and Library at PTEC provides quality information resources, library services, and learning spaces — supporting Cambodia’s educators in teaching, research, and innovation since 2017.
home.aboutAccessValue: Open access — no account, no paywall
home.authorsWorksLabel: Works by {name}, {count, plural, one {# work} other {# works}}
home.howStep1Title: Search or browse — no account needed
home.howStep1Body: Search books, theses, and publications in English or Khmer, or browse by subject. Everything is free and open.
home.howStep2Title: Read online or save for offline
home.howStep2Body: Read any PDF right in your browser, or save it to your device and keep reading without internet.
home.howStep3Body: A free account lets you save books, track reading progress, write reviews, and get alerts when new resources arrive.
home.faqQ1: Is the library free to use?
home.faqA1: Yes. The PTEC Digital Library is a free public library — reading and downloading digital resources costs nothing, and no payment is ever required.
home.faqQ2: Can I download books to read offline?
home.faqA2: Yes. Books with a PDF can be saved to your device and read without an internet connection. Your saved books are listed on the Offline books page.
home.faqA3: No account is needed to search, browse, or read. A free account adds extras: saving books, tracking your reading progress, writing reviews, and new-arrival alerts.
home.faqQ4: How do I borrow a physical book?
home.faqA4: Check Books in Library to see each title's availability and shelf location, then visit the library desk during opening hours to borrow it.
home.faqA5: Yes. The collection includes resources in both Khmer and English, and the whole website can be used in either language.
home.faqQ6: How do I submit my thesis or research?
home.faqA6: Theses are added to the repository by library staff. Contact the library team and they will guide you through submitting your work.
home.goalsTitle: What do you want to do today?
home.goalsBody: Pick a starting point — each one leads to real resources already in the library.
home.goalLessonBody: Lesson plans, teaching methods and classroom materials.
home.goalThesisBody: Past theses, research reports and writing guides.
home.goalResearchBody: Research methods, academic writing and data literacy.
home.goalTeacherBody: Guided learning paths for professional growth.
home.goalKhmerBody: Books and materials in the Khmer language.
home.weekEmpty: New highlights are being curated — check back soon.
home.popularOnboarding: Sign in free to track your reading and pick up where you left off.
home.libraryNowTitle: Read online, or visit us on campus
home.libraryNowBody: Every resource is free online, anytime. The physical library is open during these hours.
home.libraryNowDigitalBody: Read and download every resource online — free, on any device.
home.libraryNowHoursNote: Regular hours — public holidays may differ.
home.ctaBodyNoCount: Textbooks, research papers, and teaching guides — available free to every educator in Cambodia.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: The PTEC e-Library is a free digital library operated by the Phnom Penh Teacher Education College. We collect only the personal data needed to run the library, we never sell it, and you can delete your account and data at any time. This page explains exactly what we store and why.
privacy.collectAccount: Account information: your email address, display name, and an optional profile photo. If you sign in with Google, we receive your name, email, and Google profile photo.
privacy.collectActivity: Library activity: books you save, reading lists, reading progress, personal notes and highlights, and reviews you write. This is what makes features like “continue reading” work.
privacy.collectContact: Contact form messages: the name, email, and message you send us are forwarded to library staff so we can reply, and are not used for anything else.
privacy.collectTechnical: Technical records: download logs and search queries, used to keep the service secure and to improve the catalog (for example, finding searches that return no results).
privacy.notBody: We do not sell or share your personal data with advertisers, we do not show ads, and we do not track you across other websites. We do not collect any personal data beyond what is listed above.
privacy.storageBody: Account and activity data are stored in our database (Supabase/PostgreSQL); book files are stored on the library’s file storage. All connections use HTTPS encryption, and access to private data is restricted by server-side permission checks.
privacy.visibilityPrivate: Private to you: personal notes, highlights, reading progress, saved books, and reading lists are visible only to your account. Library staff cannot browse them.
privacy.visibilityPublic: Public: book reviews you post are shown on the book page together with your display name and profile photo.
privacy.retentionBody: Your data is kept while your account exists. When you delete your account, your profile, notes, reading progress, saved lists, and reviews are permanently removed. Contact-form messages are kept only as long as needed to respond.
privacy.choicesBody: You can edit your name and photo, change your password, or permanently delete your account and all associated data from
privacy.contactBody: If you have any question about your data, or want a copy of it, please reach us through the

header.subtitle: Here is what changed across the library.

toolbar.timezoneNote: Dates are inclusive and use Asia/Phnom_Penh.
toolbar.compareNote: The comparison period has the same length, immediately before the selected one.
toolbar.filterScope: Filters apply to every section, for {range}.

kpi.uniqueVisitorsDef: Distinct people who viewed a content detail page. Signed-in users are counted once across the period; anonymous visitors are counted per day using a privacy-preserving daily-rotating identifier (no IP addresses are stored).
kpi.detailViewsDef: Detail-page views of books, theses, publications and posts during the selected period. Bot traffic is excluded.
kpi.readerOpensDef: Times a visitor opened the online PDF reader. This event is newly instrumented — history begins when collection started.
kpi.downloadsDef: Completed download requests for book and thesis files during the selected period.
kpi.conversionDef: Identified visitors who opened the reader or downloaded a file, divided by identified visitors who viewed a content detail page, in the selected period.
kpi.periodNote: All five cards show activity in {range} only — lifetime inventory lives in Content → Collection health.
kpi.internalExcluded: Internal staff activity is excluded from these metrics.
kpi.visitorsDef: Distinct people who viewed a content detail page. Signed-in users are counted once across the period; anonymous visitors are counted per day using a privacy-preserving daily-rotating identifier (no IP addresses are stored).
kpi.viewsDef: Detail-page views of books, theses, publications and posts during the selected period. Bot traffic and internal staff activity are excluded.
kpi.noComparison: No previous-period baseline to compare against.
kpi.trendLegend: Solid line: this period. Dashed line: previous period.
kpi.noContentData: No content recorded this metric during the period.
kpi.visitorsLimitation: Anonymous visitors are identified by a daily-rotating hash, so one anonymous person active on several days counts once per day. This is a deliberate privacy trade-off.
kpi.collectingLimitation: This event is newly instrumented, so earlier periods have no data to compare against.

engagement.drillError: Could not load the breakdown — please try again.
engagement.visitorRollupNote: · weekly and monthly points show the busiest day, since distinct visitors cannot be summed.
engagement.eventCorrelation: {series} in the {window, plural, =1 {bucket} other {# buckets}} after this date were {pct}% versus the same span before it. This is a correlation, not a demonstrated cause.

actionCenter.title: Needs attention
actionCenter.openItems: {count, plural, =0 {no open items} =1 {1 open item} other {# open items}}
actionCenter.summary: {count, plural, =1 {1 item needs attention} other {# items need attention}}
actionCenter.allClear: All checks passed — nothing needs your attention.

items.brokenFiles: {count, plural, =1 {1 file is broken or unreachable} other {# files are broken or unreachable}}
items.storageErrors: {count, plural, =1 {1 storage request failed} other {# storage requests failed}}
items.r2Fallback: {count, plural, =1 {1 download used the legacy R2 fallback} other {# downloads used the legacy R2 fallback}}
items.aiFailures: {count, plural, =1 {1 AI request failed} other {# AI requests failed}}
items.missingMetadata: {count, plural, =1 {1 published book has missing metadata} other {# published books have missing metadata}}
items.contentDrafts: {count, plural, =1 {1 draft awaits publication} other {# drafts await publication}}
items.staleDrafts: {count, plural, =1 {1 draft is older than 30 days} other {# drafts are older than 30 days}}
items.pendingRequests: {count, plural, =1 {1 book request awaits review} other {# book requests await review}}
items.contactInbox: {count, plural, =1 {1 unread contact message} other {# unread contact messages}}
items.needsReview: {count, plural, =1 {1 item is waiting in the review queue} other {# items are waiting in the review queue}}
items.zeroResultQueries: {count, plural, =1 {1 search term repeatedly finds nothing} other {# search terms repeatedly find nothing}}
items.lowStock: {count, plural, =1 {1 catalogue title is low on copies} other {# catalogue titles are low on copies}}
items.catalogEmpty: Physical catalogue has no records yet — add your first copies

actionCenter.passed: {count, plural, =1 {1 check passed} other {# checks passed}}
actionCenter.noneClear: No checks have been evaluated yet.
actionCenter.clearExplain: These checks are currently passing. Alerts here are derived live from your data, so an item disappears as soon as the underlying problem is fixed — there is nothing to close manually.

impact.readerAttempts: {count} reader-open or download attempts hit these records this period.

explain.brokenFiles: The stored file cannot be fetched, so readers see an error instead of the document. Repair or replace the file, or unpublish the record until it is fixed.
explain.storageErrors: Storage requests returned an error or timed out. Repeated failures mean downloads and reader opens are failing for real visitors.
explain.r2Fallback: Some downloads were served from the legacy R2 bucket instead of Zima. This still works, but the records should be migrated.
explain.aiFailures: A significant share of AI requests failed. Search summaries and the assistant may be degraded.
explain.missingMetadata: Published books are missing a cover, description, author, language or department. Incomplete records rank poorly in search and look unfinished to readers.
explain.contentDrafts: Drafts are invisible to readers until published. Review them or archive the ones that are no longer needed.
explain.staleDrafts: These drafts have sat unpublished for more than 30 days — decide whether to finish or archive them.
explain.pendingRequests: Readers asked the library to acquire these titles and are waiting for a response.
explain.contactInbox: Messages sent through the public contact form have not been read.
explain.needsReview: Items are sitting in the review queue and cannot be published until someone reviews them.
explain.zeroResultQueries: Readers searched these terms repeatedly and got nothing back — either the collection lacks the material or the metadata does not match how people search.
explain.lowStock: Physical catalogue titles have one copy or fewer available to borrow.
explain.catalogEmpty: No physical copies have been recorded yet.

insights.title: Automated insights
insights.disclaimer: Generated by fixed rules over the metrics above — no AI involvement, and correlations are not causes.
insights.viewsUpConversionDown: Views grew {viewsGrowthPct}% but reader-open conversion fell from {convPrevPct}% to {convNowPct}% — more people are finding content without reading it.
insights.zeroResultRepeats: {terms} search terms repeatedly returned no results this period.
insights.newContentNoViews: {count} resources published this period have not been viewed yet.
insights.deptDemandLowCoverage: “{dept}” gets {viewsPerResource} views per resource from only {resources, plural, =1 {# resource} other {# resources}} — demand outstrips the collection.
insights.khmerSearchGrowth: Khmer searches grew to {nowPct}% of all searches (from {prevPct}%).
insights.emptyHint: Insight rules need a minimum amount of activity before they report anything, which avoids drawing conclusions from a handful of events.

why.viewsUpConversionDown: Discovery is working but the reading experience is not converting — usually a cover, description or file-access problem.
why.zeroResultRepeats: Repeated empty searches are the clearest signal of a collection or metadata gap.
why.newContentNoViews: Newly published material that nobody sees is usually a discoverability problem, not a demand problem.
why.deptDemandLowCoverage: Readers are exhausting a small part of the collection — a good candidate for acquisition budget.
why.storageFailuresUp: Storage failures translate directly into downloads and reader opens that readers never receive.
why.khmerSearchGrowth: The audience mix is shifting, which affects which language your metadata should prioritise.

action.viewsUpConversionDown: Check the top-viewed records for missing files, weak descriptions or broken readers.
action.zeroResultRepeats: Review those terms and add resources or synonyms.
action.newContentNoViews: Feature the new records or improve their metadata and keywords.
action.deptDemandLowCoverage: Consider acquiring more titles for this department.
action.storageFailuresUp: Inspect the storage log and confirm Zima is healthy.
action.khmerSearchGrowth: Prioritise Khmer titles, keywords and translations.

content.emptyPreset: No content matches this view for the selected period and filters.
content.deptFormula: Normalised as activity ÷ published resources in that department, so small departments compare fairly against large ones.
content.deptEmpty: No department activity in this period.
content.metaFormula: Metadata completeness — the share of key catalogue fields filled in (cover, description, author, language, department, year, …)

health.title: System health
health.lifetimeNote: Lifetime totals — unaffected by the date range
health.catalogEmptyHint: No physical copies are tracked yet. Add catalogue records to manage shelf stock.

levelLong.degraded: At least one check is outside its normal range.
levelLong.critical: At least one check has failed and needs attention now.
levelLong.unknown: Not enough activity in this period to judge system health.

checkDetail.brokenFiles: {value, plural, =0 {No files are broken or unreachable.} =1 {1 file is broken or unreachable.} other {# files are broken or unreachable.}}
checkDetail.storageErrors: {value}% of {sample} storage requests failed this period. Warning above 2%, critical above 10%.
checkDetail.aiFailures: {value}% of {sample} AI requests failed this period. Warning above 20%.
checkDetail.backupAge: Last successful database backup was {value} hours ago. Warning after 48 hours, critical after 7 days.

checkUnknown.brokenFiles: File health has not been probed yet.
checkUnknown.storageErrors: Only {sample} storage requests this period — too few to judge an error rate.
checkUnknown.aiFailures: Only {sample} AI requests this period — too few to judge a failure rate.
checkUnknown.backupAge: No successful backup has been recorded yet.

searchAi.kmShareHint: Share of searches typed in Khmer
searchAi.noNoClick: No ignored result sets in this period.
searchAi.aiCollecting: AI request telemetry starts collecting after this deployment — success rate and latency will appear here.
searchAi.opportunitiesHint: Deterministic rules over search demand — repeated zero-result terms and high-demand topics with thin results.
searchAi.noOpportunities: No collection gaps detected from search behaviour in this period.
searchAi.trendSubtitle: Searches per day with the zero-result share
searchAi.trendSr: {total} searches in total, {zero} returned no results.
searchAi.testQueryNote: Rows marked “suspected test” match a deterministic automation heuristic and are excluded from collection opportunities — they remain in raw analytics.
searchAi.aiCollectingShort: Collecting telemetry — success rate and latency appear after the next deployment.
searchAi.dismissFailed: Could not dismiss the term. Please try again.

audience.newRegistrationsDef: Accounts created during the selected period (not the cumulative total).
audience.activeUsersDef: Signed-in users with any view, reader or download activity during the period.
audience.returningUsersDef: Signed-in users active on two or more different days during the period.
audience.identifiedVisitorsDef: Distinct visitors countable this period: signed-in users plus anonymous daily sessions. No IP addresses or durable anonymous identifiers are stored.
audience.localeCollecting: Language tracking is newly instrumented — data appears as new views are recorded.
audience.privacyNote: Anonymous visitors are counted with a daily-rotating hash: one person returning across days counts once per day. Raw IP addresses are never stored, and device-level tracking is not performed.
audience.smallDataNote: Small dataset ({count} public detail views in this period) — read shares as indicative, not conclusive.

system.storageCollecting: Storage telemetry starts collecting after this deployment — Zima success/failure and R2 fallback usage will appear here.
system.aiCollecting: No AI requests recorded in this period yet.
system.backupNotTracked: Not tracked by this application yet
system.telemetryCollectingNote: Storage and AI telemetry begin collecting after the next deployment — statuses show “Collecting” until then.
system.backupSetupHint: Backup logging is not set up — see docs/BACKUP-DR.md

states.sectionErrorHint: The rest of the dashboard is unaffected. Retry to reload the data.
states.timezoneNote: Periods are inclusive and use Asia/Phnom_Penh.

overview.collectionLinkHint: Inventory totals, metadata gaps and the full performance table live in the Content view.

discovery.title: Engagement pathways
discovery.subtitle: How measurement connects — and where it cannot · {range}
discovery.conversionDef: Identified visitors who opened the reader or downloaded a file ÷ identified visitors who viewed a content detail page.
discovery.definition: These are independent event volumes — visitors also arrive through direct links, so the streams do not form a sequential funnel. Rates compare only pairs measured on comparable populations.
discovery.conversionShortDef: Engaged visitors ÷ visitors who viewed a detail page

pathway.searchNote: Only the search → result-click step is attributable: both events belong to the same interaction. Detail views that follow a search are not linked to it, so no further search conversion is claimed.

status.failingChecks: ({count, plural, =1 {1 check failing} other {# checks failing}})

opportunities.title: Search opportunities
opportunities.subtitle: What readers looked for and did not find · {range}
opportunities.methodology: Terms searched at least 3 times in the period, ranked by volume weighted by how poorly they are served. Suspected test queries are excluded.
opportunities.emptyHint: Every repeated search in this period returned results that readers clicked.

recommend.zeroResult: Add a resource, or a synonym if the material exists under another name.
recommend.lowCoverage: Expand the collection for this topic.
recommend.lowClickThrough: Results exist but readers skip them — review titles, covers and ranking.

contentPanel.title: Content performance

adminCatalogCover.generatedInfo: Nothing is stored. Readers see this PTEC cover, generated from the book's title, author and category.

adminAnnouncements.title: Announcements
adminAnnouncements.description: Create, schedule, and track bilingual announcements across in-app, banner, and push channels.
adminAnnouncements.readOnlyNotice: You have view-only access to announcements. Ask an administrator for write access to create or edit them.
adminAnnouncements.noPushPermissionNotice: You can create and edit announcements, but sending a push notification requires additional permission (Announcement Push).

metrics.heading: Summary metrics

metrics.draftsHint: Draft and awaiting-approval announcements not yet published.
metrics.subscribersHint: Devices currently subscribed to browser/PWA push notifications.
metrics.deliveryRateHint: Share of push attempts accepted by the browser push provider in the selected period.

filters.searchPlaceholder: Search title, internal name, or content…
filters.resultCount: {count, plural, =0 {No announcements} one {# announcement} other {# announcements}}

empty.noneDescription: Create your first announcement to reach readers in-app, on the website banner, or via push notification.
empty.noResultsDescription: Try a different search term or reset the filters.

toasts.failed: Something went wrong. Please try again.
toasts.noRetryable: No retryable failed deliveries right now.
toasts.deleteDescriptionSingle: "{name}" will be permanently removed. This cannot be undone.
toasts.deleteDescriptionBulk: These drafts will be permanently removed. This cannot be undone.
toasts.deleteHint: Only drafts can be deleted — archive published announcements instead to keep their delivery history.

composer.createDescription: Build one bilingual announcement, choose its channels and audience, then review before sending.
composer.editDescription: Changes are saved to this draft until you publish or schedule it.

content.internalNamePlaceholder: e.g. Library closed — Khmer New Year 2027
content.internalNameHelp: For admin use only — never shown to readers.

priorityHelp.important: Highlighted styling; still a normal confirmation to send.
priorityHelp.urgent: Strong visual emphasis; large push broadcasts require typed confirmation.

content.imageBadType: Please choose a JPEG, PNG, WebP, or AVIF image.
content.imageTooLarge: Image must be 5 MB or smaller.
content.ctaUrlHelp: Internal paths are always safe. External links must be https:// and on the approved domain list.

channels.intro: Choose delivery channels independently — publishing in-app content never automatically sends a push notification.
channels.inAppDescription: Shown in the notification bell for eligible signed-in readers.
channels.bannerDescription: A dismissible banner shown on public pages while the announcement is active.
channels.pushDescription: Sent to subscribed devices. Requires a separate confirmation before a real broadcast.
channels.pushNoPermission: You don't have permission to send push notifications — ask an admin to publish this channel.
channels.pushTitlePlaceholder: Falls back to the English title
channels.pushBodyPlaceholder: Falls back to the English summary
channels.testSent: Test sent to {count, plural, one {# device} other {# devices}}.
channels.testFailed: Could not send a test notification.
channels.testHint: Test notifications go only to your own subscribed device and are clearly labeled "[Test]" — never to the real audience.

typeDescription.all_active: Every reader with an active (non-disabled, non-blocked) account.
typeDescription.role: Only users whose account role matches your selection.
typeDescription.push_enabled: Only users who currently have at least one active push subscription.

audience.estimateDisclaimer: This is an estimate. The real audience is recalculated on the server immediately before publishing.

modeDescription.now: Goes live as soon as you confirm on the review step.
modeDescription.schedule: Automatically published at the date/time you choose below.

schedule.expiresAtHelp: The announcement automatically stops showing after this time — its history is kept, nothing is deleted.
schedule.pinnedHelp: Pinned announcements are shown first, but never more than a few at once to avoid banner fatigue.
schedule.dismissibleHelp: Turn off only for announcements that must stay visible (e.g. emergency notices).

review.warnMissingKm: No Khmer title yet — Khmer readers will see the English version.
review.warnTitleTruncated: The English title is long and may be truncated on some surfaces.
review.warnBadLink: The destination link isn't valid or isn't on the approved domain list.
review.warnCtaNoLink: A call-to-action label is set without a destination link.
review.warnNoImage: No image set — some layouts show a placeholder instead.
review.warnBannerRestrictedAudience: The website banner has no way to check who is viewing, so it can only show to all active users — it will not appear at all for this restricted audience. Use in-app or push for a targeted audience.
review.noPushPermissionWarning: Push is selected, but you don't have permission to send it — ask an admin to publish.
review.confirmBody: You are about to publish this announcement and send a push notification to approximately {devices} subscribed device(s). Push notifications cannot be recalled after delivery.
review.confirmIrreversible: In-app and banner content can be paused any time; a push that has already been delivered to a device cannot be.
review.confirmTypeSend: Type SEND to confirm this urgent / large broadcast

toasts.publishing: Publishing — sending to about {devices} device(s) now.

detail.noDeliveryJobs: No push delivery has been attempted yet.
detail.deliveryHonestyNote: "Sent" means the push provider accepted the notification for delivery — it does not guarantee the device displayed it.
detail.creator: Creator
detail.archiveConfirmDescription: It will stop showing on every channel. Its delivery history and analytics are kept.

toasts.failed: Something went wrong. Please try again.
toasts.retryQueued: {count, plural, =0 {Nothing to retry} one {# delivery queued for retry} other {# deliveries queued for retry}}

templates.title: Announcement templates

templates.emptyDescription: Save a template for messages you send often, like library closures or event reminders.

adminReview.title: Review Queue
adminReview.description: Books and theses in the verification workflow. Approving verifies the metadata and publishes; “verify only” marks it checked without publishing. Editors cannot verify their own records.

noteBox.label: What needs to change?
noteBox.placeholder: e.g. Author name doesn't match the title page; publication year missing

details.unverifiedNote: Shown with an “unverified” notice on public pages and excluded from repository feeds until verified.

versions.none: No recorded changes yet (history starts once migration 0086 is applied).
versions.restoreDescription: The current values are snapshotted first, so this can be undone.

empty.title: Nothing waiting for review

noResults.title: No paths match your filters

bulkArchiveDialog.title: Archive selected paths?
bulkArchiveDialog.description: {count} path(s) will be hidden from the public site and unpublished. Learner progress is kept and they can be restored later.

deleteDialog.title: Delete this learning path?
deleteDialog.description: “{title}” will be permanently deleted, along with all of its modules, steps, and every learner's enrollment and progress. This cannot be undone — consider archiving instead.

scheduleDialog.title: Schedule publication
scheduleDialog.description: Set when “{title}” should go live. It stays hidden until an editor publishes it at or after this time.

fields.subject: Subject / category

builder.checklistOk: Everything looks good — ready to publish.

validation.stepNoUrl: Add a URL for every external-link step in “{title}”.

validation.badDuration: Estimated duration must be a positive number.
validation.noSchedule: Choose a publish date to schedule.

toasts.duplicated: Learning path duplicated as a draft.
toasts.error: Something went wrong — please try again.

adminDuplicates.title: Duplicate Review
adminDuplicates.description: Probable duplicate records, grouped by shared ISBN, PDF file identity, or matching title with a corroborating signal. Nothing is merged automatically — pick the record to keep, then retire the others. Retiring archives the duplicate (its reviews and analytics are preserved) and adds a permanent 301 redirect from its URL to the record you keep.

empty.title: No probable duplicates found
empty.description: No published books currently share an ISBN, PDF file, or a matching title with a corroborating signal.

retireDialog.title: Retire this duplicate?
retireDialog.description: “{title}” will be archived and its URL will permanently redirect to the record you keep. Reviews and analytics are preserved.

toasts.failed: Could not retire the record — please try again.

adminDataQuality.title: Data quality
adminDataQuality.description: Inspect published records, repair missing metadata, and resolve files that readers cannot open.

alert.title: Metadata quality could not be calculated
alert.body: Check the database connection and schema before treating this dashboard as a clean result.

sweep.noBaseline: No file links have been checked yet. Run a sweep to establish a baseline.
sweep.applyMigration: Apply the file-health migration before running a link sweep.

gaps.title: Metadata repair queue
gaps.subtitle: Lowest completeness first, so high-impact edits stay at the top.
gaps.completeBody: New gaps will appear here automatically.

files.title: File repairs
files.subtitle: Links confirmed broken in the latest recorded checks.
files.noBaselineBody: Run the checker before treating this as a clean result.

backfill.title: Canonical backfill integrity
backfill.subtitle: Legacy vs canonical counts per domain (migrations 0104–0109). Any gap is a row the backfill missed.
backfill.unavailable: Apply the canonical-resource migrations (0104–0109) to enable this check.
backfill.footnote: Gap = legacy source rows minus canonical rows produced. It must be 0 for every domain.

adminSearchInsights.title: Search Analytics
adminSearchInsights.description: Search behavior from the last 30 days: what people look for, where search fails, which results earn clicks, and how Khmer and English usage compares.

lists.emptyMissing: No failed searches to review yet.

clicked.title: Most clicked results

actionCenter.title: Zero-result action center
actionCenter.description: Failed searches grouped by spelling. Resolve each one: map a synonym, pin a curated result, raise an acquisition request, or dismiss bot noise.
actionCenter.emptyNone: No zero-result searches in this window.

synonymPanel.note: Applied only when “{term}” finds zero results — normal ranking never changes.

upload.formatNote: Google Drive share links in cover_url are converted automatically. Comma, semicolon and tab delimiters are detected. Column names don’t need to match exactly — you’ll map them in the next step.

mapping.caption: Column mapping
mapping.footnote: Columns marked “Check match” were guessed from a similar name — confirm they point to the right field. Ignored columns are not imported.

validate.validating: Validating rows and checking the catalog for duplicates…

progress.cancelling: Cancelling — the current batch will finish safely, and everything already imported stays in the catalog.
progress.keepOpen: Keep this window open. Books are written in small batches — cancelling keeps completed batches.
progress.cancelledNote: Batches finished before cancelling were imported — the counts below are exact.
